advancement.mineraculous.adventure.perch: Perch above the top of the world (build limit) using the Cat Staff
advancement.mineraculous.husbandry.obtai: What are they feeding you to?
advancement.mineraculous.miraculous.cata: Cataclysm a block and watch it spread
Cataclysm an entity that dies before it is cured
advancement.mineraculous.miraculous.kami: Give power to another being via kamikotization
Use the butterfly miraculous to kamikotize yourself
advancement.mineraculous.miraculous.obta: It's me, hi, I'm the problem, it's me
advancement.mineraculous.miraculous.root: Just a normal player, with a normal life
advancement.mineraculous.miraculous.tran: Accept a kamikotization from the Butterfly miraculous holder
block.minecraft.bed.mineraculous.stealin: You may not rest now, there are thieves nearby.
block.mineraculous.waxed_time_honored_ca: Waxed Block of Time Honored Camembert
block.mineraculous.waxed_time_honored_ch: Waxed Block of Time Honored Cheese
book.mineraculous.wiki.apis.abilities.de: Powerful entity enhancements that can be used by Miraculous or Kamikotization holders.
book.mineraculous.wiki.apis.abilities.ge:
Abilities do not have a standalone generator. Instead, they are defined inline directly within Miraculous and Kamikotization definitions!
You can configure abilities using the online [Miraculous Generator](https://beta-jsons.thomasglasser.dev/mineraculous/miraculous/) or [Kamikotization Generator](https://beta-jsons.thomasglasser.dev/mineraculous/kamikotization/).
book.mineraculous.wiki.apis.abilities.st:
Abilities are structured using conditions and branches.
Branches contain actions that execute when their conditions are met.
Conditions can check properties of the performer, source, or target.
Some example actions include:
- mineraculous:apply_effects_or_destroy
- mineraculous:convert_and_tame
- mineraculous:passive_infinite_effects
- mineraculous:replace_adjacent_blocks
- mineraculous:replace_item_in_main_hand
- mineraculous:revert_lucky_charm_targets_ability_effects
- mineraculous:right_hand_particles
- mineraculous:spectate_entity
- mineraculous:summon_target_dependent_lucky_charm
- mineraculous:toggleable_special_effects
book.mineraculous.wiki.apis.advancement_: Advancement triggers for addon mods and datapacks to detect and use.
A generator for advancements can be found online [here](https://beta-jsons.thomasglasser.dev/advancement/).

This trigger is called when a player kamikotizes an entity.
It has four parameters:
- "player": The player that kamikotized the entity.
- "target": The entity that was kamikotized.
- "kamikotization": The kamikotization that was given to the entity.
- "self": Whether the player kamikotized themself.

This trigger is called when a player performs an ability.
It has four optional parameters:
- "player": The player that performed the ability.
- "id": The ID of the ability that was performed.
- "source": A predicate checking the source of the ability (such as a Miraculous or Kamikotization).
- "target": A predicate checking the target of the ability.

This trigger is called when a player releases purified entities.
It has three parameters:
- "player": The player that released the entities.
- "released": The entities that were released.
- "count": The number of purified entities that were released.

This trigger is called when a player is transformed by a kamikotization.
It has three parameters:
- "player": The player that was transformed.
- "kamikotization": The kamikotization that was used to transform.
- "self": Whether the player kamikotized themself.

This trigger is called when a player transforms with a miraculous.
It has two parameters:
- "player": The player that transformed.
- "miraculous": The miraculous that was used to transform.
book.mineraculous.wiki.apis.data_maps.ag:
There is an ageable data map for blocks implementing the AgeingCheese interface that determines the block to change to when ageing.
This is located at "data/mineraculous/data_maps/block/ageables.json".
A generator for this can be found online [here](https://beta-jsons.thomasglasser.dev/mineraculous/data-map-ageables/).
book.mineraculous.wiki.apis.data_maps.de: Data-driven registry element maps used to expand mod functionality.
book.mineraculous.wiki.apis.data_maps.lu:
Contextual lucky charms are determined for kamikotizations, miraculouses, and entities via data maps.
They are located in "data/mineraculous/data_maps/mineraculous/kamikotization/lucky_charms.json",
"data/mineraculous/data_maps/mineraculous/miraculous/lucky_charms.json",
and "data/mineraculous/data_maps/entity_type/lucky_charms.json" respectively.
Generators for these can be found online [here](https://beta-jsons.thomasglasser.dev/partners/).
Generators for the loot table can be found [here](https://beta-jsons.thomasglasser.dev/loot-table/).
*Note: At this time, to generate a lucky charm loot table, you must use a preset to set the "type" field to "mineraculous:lucky_charm".
Searching the presets for "lucky_charm" will yield valid results.*
book.mineraculous.wiki.apis.data_maps.mi:
Miraculous effects and attributes are provided to miraculous holders and kamikotized entities while transformed.
These are determined via two data maps:
- "data/mineraculous/data_maps/mob_effect/miraculous_effects.json" for mob effects ([Generator](https://beta-jsons.thomasglasser.dev/mineraculous/data-map-miraculous-effects/))
- "data/mineraculous/data_maps/attribute/miraculous_attribute_modifiers.json" for attributes ([Generator](https://beta-jsons.thomasglasser.dev/mineraculous/data-map-miraculous-attribute-modifiers/))
book.mineraculous.wiki.apis.description: APIs that allow for Java and JSON addon customization.
book.mineraculous.wiki.apis.kamikotizati: Transformations that the Butterfly Miraculous holder can give to entities.
A generator for kamikotizations can be found [here](https://beta-jsons.thomasglasser.dev/mineraculous/kamikotization/).
A guide for creating kamikotizations can be found [here](https://beta-jsons.thomasglasser.dev/guides/kamikotization/).
book.mineraculous.wiki.apis.looks.genera: A generator for looks can be found [here](https://beta-jsons.thomasglasser.dev/mineraculous/look/).
A guide for creating looks can be found [here](https://beta-jsons.thomasglasser.dev/guides/look/).
book.mineraculous.wiki.apis.looks.name: Looks
book.mineraculous.wiki.apis.loot.conditi:
There is 1 loot condition added by the mod:
- "mineraculous:has_item": Checks if the provided entity has an item matching the provided predicate in their inventory.
book.mineraculous.wiki.apis.loot.descrip: Extensions of the loot table system.
book.mineraculous.wiki.apis.loot.functio:
There are 2 loot functions added by the mod:
- "mineraculous:dye_randomly": Dyes the provided stack with the provided number of random colors.
- "mineraculous:set_miraculous_randomly": Sets a compatible miraculous randomly to the provided stack.
book.mineraculous.wiki.apis.loot.name: Loot
book.mineraculous.wiki.apis.loot.number_:
There is one number provider added by the mod:
- "mineraculous:power_level_multiplier": Generates a number with the provided Number Provider and multiplies it by the provided power level parameter divided by the provided divisor.
book.mineraculous.wiki.apis.miraculouses: Wearable items that provide special abilities and visuals to the holder.
A generator for miraculouses can be found [here](https://beta-jsons.thomasglasser.dev/mineraculous/miraculous/).
A guide for creating miraculouses can be found [here](https://beta-jsons.thomasglasser.dev/guides/miraculous/).
book.mineraculous.wiki.apis.name: APIs
book.mineraculous.wiki.apis.recipe_seria:
The "mineraculous:crafting_special_cheesewedge" serializer is a special crafting recipe for cheese blocks and wedges.
It allows combining cheese wedges back into full blocks or breaking blocks down into individual wedges in a crafting grid.
Custom recipe formats for new and existing processing blocks.
A generator for recipes can be found online [here](https://beta-jsons.thomasglasser.dev/recipe/).

The oven cooking recipe serializer is used with the Oven block.
It has the same format as the other cooking recipes.

Smelting, smoking, campfire cooking, and oven cooking recipes have transmute variants.
They provide an item result instead of an ItemStack and transmute components to the result.
book.mineraculous.wiki.apis.tags.biome.t:
There are 5 mod tags used by the mod:
- "mineraculous:has_common_almond_trees": Biomes with common almond trees.
- "mineraculous:has_rare_almond_trees": Biomes with rare almond trees.
- "mineraculous:has_warm_variant_hibiscus": Biomes with warm variant hibiscus.
- "mineraculous:has_temperate_variant_hibiscus": Biomes with temperate variant hibiscus.
- "mineraculous:has_cold_variant_hibiscus": Biomes with cold variant hibiscus.
book.mineraculous.wiki.apis.tags.block.t:
There are 4 mod tags and 1 common tag used by the mod:
- "mineraculous:cataclysm_immune": Blocks that are unable to be replaced by Cataclysm.
- "mineraculous:cheese_blocks": Blocks that are normal cheese.
- "mineraculous:camembert_blocks": Blocks that are camembert cheese.
- "mineraculous:hibiscus_bushes": Blocks that are hibiscus bushes.
- "c:foods/cheese_blocks": Blocks from any mod that can be considered a cheese block and food.
book.mineraculous.wiki.apis.tags.damage_:
There are 2 mod tags used by the mod:
- "mineraculous:hurts_kamikos": Damage types that can hurt Kamikos.
- "mineraculous:is_cataclysm": Damage types that can be considered cataclysm.
book.mineraculous.wiki.apis.tags.descrip: Registry element lists used for mod functionality.
book.mineraculous.wiki.apis.tags.entity_:
There are 2 mod tags and 1 common tag used by the mod:
- "mineraculous:cataclysm_immune": Entities that cannot be affected by Cataclysm.
- "mineraculous:ladybug_yoyo_extended_range": Entities that can be hit from a further distance by the Thrown Ladybug Yoyo.
- "c:butterflies": Entities from any mod that can be considered a butterfly.
book.mineraculous.wiki.apis.tags.generat: Generators for vanilla tags can be found online [here](https://beta-jsons.thomasglasser.dev/generators/) and mod tags can be found online [here](https://beta-jsons.thomasglasser.dev/partners/).
book.mineraculous.wiki.apis.tags.item.te:
There are 19 mod tags and 2 common tags used by the mod:
- "mineraculous:miraculous_transformers": Items that are a miraculous players can transform with.
- "mineraculous:kwami_preferred_foods/butterfly": Items that can be used to have a better chance to charge the butterfly kwami.
- "mineraculous:kwami_treats/butterfly": Items that can be used to immediately charge the butterfly kwami.
- "mineraculous:kwami_preferred_foods/cat": Items that can be used to have a better chance to charge the cat kwami.
- "mineraculous:kwami_treats/cat": Items that can be used to immediately charge the cat kwami.
- "mineraculous:kwami_preferred_foods/ladybug": Items that can be used to have a better chance to charge the ladybug kwami.
- "mineraculous:kwami_treats/ladybug": Items that can be used to immediately charge the ladybug kwami.
- "mineraculous:cheese": Items that are normal cheese.
- "mineraculous:cheese_blocks": An item copy of the block tag.
- "mineraculous:camembert": Items that are camembert cheese.
- "mineraculous:camembert_blocks": An item copy of the block tag.
- "mineraculous:cataclysm_immune": Items that are unable to be replaced by Cataclysm.
- "mineraculous:tough": Items that take two tries to break if they do not have a max damage value.
- "mineraculous:lucky_charm_shader_immune": Items that do not have a visual change when given the "mineraculous:lucky_charm" component.
- "mineraculous:shooting_projectiles": Projectile Items that shoot down from the Lucky Charm Spawner instead of dropping normally.
- "mineraculous:generic_lucky_charms": Lucky charm options when no specific pool is selected.
- "mineraculous:warden_distractors": Items passed in a Warden lucky charm to distract it.
- "mineraculous:kamikotization_immune": Items that cannot be used for Kamikotization.
- "mineraculous:hibiscus": Items that are hibiscus.
- "c:foods/cheeses": Items from any mod that can be considered cheese and food.
- "c:foods/cheese_blocks": An item copy of the block tag.
book.mineraculous.wiki.apis.tags.miracul:
There are 3 mod tags used by the mod:
- "mineraculous:can_use_butterfly_cane": Miraculouses that can use and store data for the Butterfly Cane.
- "mineraculous:can_use_cat_staff": Miraculouses that can use and store data for the Cat Staff.
- "mineraculous:can_use_ladybug_yoyo": Miraculouses that can use and store data for the Ladybug Yoyo.
book.mineraculous.wiki.apis.tags.name: Tags
book.mineraculous.wiki.cosmetics.beta.co:
Beta Cosmetics have three client configuration options:
- "display_self_beta_tester_cosmetic" - Whether or not to display your own Beta Tester cosmetic.
- "beta_tester_cosmetic_choice" - The beta tester cosmetic to display.
- "display_others_beta_tester_cosmetic" - Whether or not to display other players' Beta Tester cosmetics.
book.mineraculous.wiki.cosmetics.beta.de: Exclusive cosmetics for members of the Beta Program.
book.mineraculous.wiki.cosmetics.beta.se: Testers for 1.0.0 (Season 1) get access to a Derby Hat cosmetic.
book.mineraculous.wiki.cosmetics.descrip: Exclusive visual-only benefits for special community members.
book.mineraculous.wiki.cosmetics.dev.con:
Dev Team Cosmetics have four client configuration options:
- "display_self_dev_team_cosmetic" - Whether or not to display your own Dev Team cosmetic.
- "display_self_legacy_dev_team_cosmetic" - Whether or not to display your own Legacy Dev Team cosmetic.
- "display_others_dev_team_cosmetic" - Whether or not to display other players' Dev Team cosmetics.
- "display_others_legacy_dev_team_cosmetic" - Whether or not to display other players' Legacy Dev Team cosmetics.
book.mineraculous.wiki.cosmetics.dev.des: Exclusive cosmetics for members of the Development Team.
book.mineraculous.wiki.cosmetics.dev.leg: Members of the development team that joined the project before the release of 1.0.0 get access to an exclusive beard cosmetic.
book.mineraculous.wiki.cosmetics.name: Cosmetics
book.mineraculous.wiki.decorations.armor: The Butterfly armor trim can be found in Desert Pyramid archeology.
The Cat armor trim can be found in Desert Pyramid archeology.
Armor trim items that can be applied to armor for cosmetic purposes.
The Ladybug armor trim can be found in Desert Pyramid archeology.
book.mineraculous.wiki.decorations.banne: The Butterfly banner pattern can be found in Desert Well archeology.
The Cat banner pattern can be found in Desert Well archeology.
Banner pattern items that can be used to customize banners.
The Ladybug banner pattern can be found in Desert Well archeology.
book.mineraculous.wiki.decorations.descr: Custom paintings, armor trims, banner patterns, and pottery sherds inspired by miraculous folklore to personalize and decorate your world.
book.mineraculous.wiki.decorations.name: Decorations
book.mineraculous.wiki.decorations.paint: Decorative paintings to hang on your walls.
The mod adds a variety of placeable paintings that can be used to brighten up your world.
book.mineraculous.wiki.decorations.potte: The Butterfly pottery sherd can be found in Desert Pyramid archeology.
The Cat pottery sherd can be found in Desert Pyramid archeology.
Pottery sherd items that can be used to craft Decorated Pots.
The Ladybug pottery sherd can be found in Desert Pyramid archeology.
book.mineraculous.wiki.dependencies.desc: Mods that are not required but enhance the Mineraculous experience.
book.mineraculous.wiki.dependencies.jade: Jade is a block and entity information viewing mod.
The [Jade](https://modrinth.com/mod/jade) overlay allows you to view the contents and baking progress of the [Oven](entry://food/workstations).
Just Enough Items is an item and recipe viewing mod.
[Just Enough Items](https://modrinth.com/mod/jei) is able to recognize and show all data-driven Miraculouses and custom recipes.
book.mineraculous.wiki.dependencies.modo: Modonomicon is an in-game documentation mod.
[Modonomicon](https://modrinth.com/mod/modonomicon) powers the very in-game guide book you are reading right now!
book.mineraculous.wiki.dependencies.name: Optional Dependencies
book.mineraculous.wiki.dependencies.voic: Simple Voice Chat is a voice and proximity chat mod.

When [Simple Voice Chat](https://modrinth.com/mod/simple-voice-chat) is installed, private telepathic communication between a [Butterfly Miraculous](entry://miraculouses/butterfly) holder and their kamikotized targets applies to both text chat and voice chat!
This allows players to communicate across any distance without nearby players hearing their secret plans.
book.mineraculous.wiki.description:
Thank you for installing Mineraculous!
Within these pages is everything you'll ever need to know about the mod,
from how to obtain a miraculous to how to use the many powers it grants you.
Check back here when new updates come out to see the changes!
book.mineraculous.wiki.expansions.akumat: The Akumatization Pack replaces all references to "Kamiko" and "Kamikotization" with "Akuma" and "Akumatization".
book.mineraculous.wiki.expansions.descri: Official optional mods and packs that enhance the Mineraculous experience.
book.mineraculous.wiki.expansions.name: Expansions
book.mineraculous.wiki.flora_and_fauna.d: The plants, trees, and animals that make up the Mineraculous world.
book.mineraculous.wiki.flora_and_fauna.f: The vibrant florals of the mod.

Hibiscus comes in four color variants: orange, blue, purple, and pink.
Once obtained, it can be planted to grow a hibiscus bush.
Hibiscus can be harvested from a mature bush by right-clicking or breaking it.
Hibiscus is also the treat for the [Butterfly Kwami](entry://miraculouses/butterfly).

Sniffers are the primary way to find hibiscus flowers in the wild.
By foraging through grassy or dirt terrain, they can dig up hibiscus of varying colors.
The color discovered depends on the surrounding biome, making sniffers invaluable for collectors seeking rare variants.
book.mineraculous.wiki.flora_and_fauna.t:
Almond trees are found in savanna and badlands biomes.
They can be grown from Almond Saplings, which drop from Almond Leaves.
Almond Leaves also occasionally drop raw almonds, making these trees a useful food source as well.
The towering timber of the mod.

Almond trees come with a full wood set, including logs, planks, slabs, stairs, fences, and more.
Almond wood has a warm brown appearance that pairs well with savanna and mesa builds.
Almond Boats are also available for traversing waterways.
book.mineraculous.wiki.food.almonds.desc: A crunchy, natural snack dropped from Almond trees.
book.mineraculous.wiki.food.almonds.eati:
Almonds can be eaten raw but provide very little nutrition.
Roasting them increases nutrition and saturation for a more worthwhile food.
book.mineraculous.wiki.food.almonds.find: Almonds drop from Almond Leaves found on [Almond trees](entry://flora_and_fauna/trees) in savannas and badlands.
book.mineraculous.wiki.food.almonds.name: Almonds
book.mineraculous.wiki.food.cheese.agein:
Cheese ages slowly over time in 5 stages:
- Fresh
- Aged
- Ripened
- Exquisite
- Time-Honored\
Cheese blocks can be waxed with Honeycomb to prevent further ageing.
They can be scraped with an Axe to start ageing again.
book.mineraculous.wiki.food.cheese.camem:
Camembert is a white, creamy, and *very* stinky cheese.
It gets darker and more potent with age, and is the absolute favorite treat for the [Cat Kwami](entry://miraculouses/cat)!
book.mineraculous.wiki.food.cheese.chees: Normal cheese is a nice orange and gets darker with age.
book.mineraculous.wiki.food.cheese.descr: A stinky, delectable milk product found in village Creameries.
book.mineraculous.wiki.food.cheese.eatin:
Wedges can be eaten in item form on right click.
Blocks can be eaten by right clicking the block and eating a piece at a time.
You can add bites back to a block by right clicking it with a matching wedge, or combine wedges back into blocks within a crafting grid!
Conversely, full cheese blocks can be placed into a crafting grid to divide them into 4 individual wedges.
Blocks will also output a comparator signal that changes based on how many bites they have left.
book.mineraculous.wiki.food.cheese.findi: Cheese can be found in village Creameries and traded from the Fromager.
book.mineraculous.wiki.food.cheese.name: Cheese
book.mineraculous.wiki.food.description: The consumables, good or bad, that can be found in the world.
book.mineraculous.wiki.food.macarons.des: An almond-based sweet that can be crafted or found in village Bakeries.
book.mineraculous.wiki.food.macarons.dye:
Raw macarons can be dyed limitlessly, with each dye adding to the color of the macaron.
Once cooked, the color is permanent and cannot be changed.
book.mineraculous.wiki.food.macarons.eat:
Macarons can be eaten raw, but this will provide very little nutrition and has a chance to make the consumer sick.
When cooked in an [Oven](entry://food/workstations), they provide much more nutrition and are completely safe to eat.
Cooked macarons are also one of the favorite treats for the [Ladybug Kwami](entry://miraculouses/ladybug)!
book.mineraculous.wiki.food.macarons.fin:
Raw macarons can be crafted from an almond, sugar, and an egg.
They can then be cooked in any cooking block.
Cooked macarons also generate in village Bakeries.
book.mineraculous.wiki.food.name: Food
book.mineraculous.wiki.food.workstations:
The Cheese Pot can be found in village Creameries.
It serves as the workstation for the Fromager villager profession, allowing villagers to trade delicious cheeses and camembert.
Appliances and workstations used for culinary creations and villager professions.

The Oven is a specialized cooking appliance that operates faster than a standard furnace.
It is used to bake raw macarons into delicious cooked macarons.
Additionally, the Oven serves as the job site block for the Baker villager profession found in village Bakeries.
book.mineraculous.wiki.getting_started.d: Your essential step-by-step guide to stepping into the world of Mineraculous.
book.mineraculous.wiki.getting_started.f: Where to uncover magical Miraculouses and replica costume suits in your world.

In standard survival gameplay, true magical Miraculouses are incredibly rare endgame artifacts.
They can be discovered within treasure chests inside **End Cities**!
Because of their immense cosmic power, braving the dangers of the End dimension is required to claim your first Miraculous.
Where to Find Them

Long before reaching the End, adventurous explorers delving into ocean depths can discover [Fake Miraculouses and Costume Suits](entry://items/costumes)!
Found within **Underwater Ruin Chests** and **Ocean Ruin Archaeology** (suspicious sand and gravel), these cosmetic replicas carry no magical powers or kwamis, but let you dress up in iconic costume suits right from the start!
Replica Costumes

While End City treasure is the default source, Mineraculous is built to be fully data-driven!
Server administrators and modpack creators can configure custom loot tables, villager trades, or crafting recipes to make Miraculouses obtainable through different progression paths in their worlds.
Modpack Customization
Finding Miraculouses

Your signature tool (such as a Yoyo, Staff, or Cane) is your versatile multi-mode gadget, capable of combat, defensive blocking, and traversal.
- **Open Item Menu**: Press to quickly retrieve or stash your tool in your inventory (if supported by that tool).
- **Tool Modes**: Hold while holding your tool to open a radial menu and switch between modes like Travel, Spyglass, or Shield Block!
- **De/Activate**: Toggle tool activation while in hand (if supported by that tool).
Step 3: Signature Tools
A step-by-step tutorial on transforming, fighting, using tools, and recharging your Kwami.

Once you have obtained a [Miraculous](entry://miraculouses/general), place it into its designated Curios slot. You can equip it by right-clicking while holding it, or by shift-clicking it into the Curios inventory.
As soon as you wear it, your companion spirit, the [Kwami](entry://miraculouses/kwamis), will materialize beside you!
Step 1: Equipping
First Transformation & Combat

When your timer runs out (if below Power Level 100) or when you manually detransform, your Kwami will reappear exhausted and hungry!
You must feed your Kwami their favorite food before you can transform again:
- **[Ladybug Kwami](entry://miraculouses/kwamis)**: Cooked [Macarons](entry://food/macarons)
- **[Cat Kwami](entry://miraculouses/kwamis)**: [Cheese or Camembert](entry://food/cheese)
- **[Butterfly Kwami](entry://miraculouses/kwamis)**: [Hibiscus Flowers](entry://flora_and_fauna/flowers)
Step 5: Recharging

During tough battles, press the **Activate Power** key to unleash your Miraculous's ultimate special ability (such as Lucky Charm or Cataclysm)!
Be warned: if your [Power Level](entry://miraculouses/general) is below 100, using this ability consumes massive cosmic energy and initiates your detransformation countdown timer.
Step 4: Special Abilities

With your Miraculous worn and your Kwami charged, press the **Transform** key.
Your Kwami will merge into your jewel, clothing you in a protective magical suit! You will immediately gain supernatural attribute buffs (speed, jump boost, resistance, and strength) and receive your signature tool.
Step 2: Transforming
book.mineraculous.wiki.getting_started.k:
When the Kamiko enters an item, the target receives a [Kamikotization](entry://kamikotizations/kamikotizations_general)!
Depending on the specific power granted (such as custom powers added by datapacks or canonical show powers included in the **Kamikotization Expansion** addon), the target is transformed into an empowered player, receiving specialized abilities, boosted attributes, and custom armor or tools to assist the Butterfly Miraculous holder.

If you find yourself facing an opponent empowered by a Kamiko, simply fighting them head-on can be difficult!
To strip a kamikotized opponent of their powers, you must focus your attacks on their **possessed item** or tool. Once the item takes enough damage and breaks, the kamikotization is revoked!
How the Butterfly Miraculous empowers targets and how to overcome kamikotized foes.

When a kamikotized item is destroyed, the released Kamiko will attempt to flutter away.
If you are transformed with the [Ladybug Miraculous](entry://miraculouses/ladybug), you can use your Yoyo to capture and purify the escaping Kamiko, cleansing it of energy and releasing a normal, harmless butterfly back into the sky!

The [Butterfly Miraculous](entry://miraculouses/butterfly) commands the power of Transmission.
A transformed Butterfly holder can infuse a butterfly with cosmic energy, creating a [Kamiko](entry://getting_started/kamikos), and send it to possess an item belonging to any player!

In *Miraculous* TV show lore, power transmission relies on intentions and emotions, differentiating between "Kamikos" and villainous "Akumas".
In **Mineraculous**, the concepts of emotions, good, and evil are entirely removed; players and entities are completely neutral!
Therefore, this mod uses "Kamiko" and "Kamikotization" across all items and interfaces as the neutral terminology for empowering targets without implying villainy or emotional manipulation.
If you prefer the classic TV show terminology, you can enable the built-in [Akumatization Pack](entry://expansions/akumatization) in your Resource Packs menu to rename them to Akuma and Akumatization!
book.mineraculous.wiki.getting_started.w:
As a Miraculous holder, you will experience several unique gameplay systems:
- **Attribute Buffs**: Passive bonuses to speed, jump height, resistance, and strength.
- **Signature Tools**: Versatile tools with multi-mode utility.
- **Special Abilities**: Earth-shattering powers like Lucky Charm or Cataclysm.
- **Power Levels**: A dynamic progression system where your powers scale and improve as you gain experience!
An introduction to the magical world of Miraculouses, Kwamis, and supernatural powers.

Welcome to Mineraculous, a mod that introduces magical jewelry known as Miraculouses into your Minecraft world!
Whether you are a new player or a veteran adventurer, this guide will help you understand the core mechanics and master your new abilities.

Miraculouses are magical jewels charged with ancient power. Each jewel is tied to a specific cosmic concept (such as Creation, Destruction, or Transmission) and is inhabited by a divine companion spirit known as a [Kwami](entry://miraculouses/kwamis).
When worn, these jewels allow you to transform and channel incredible supernatural powers!
book.mineraculous.wiki.item_stealing_and:
When a player or entity has been kamikotized, their powers are bound to an item in their possession.
You can break these items by holding or pressing the Take/Break Item key while targeting them or holding the object.
The item will take damage based on its durability, hardness, or toughness until it breaks, releasing any trapped Kamiko inside so it can be purified!
Some objects (like bedrock) are completely immune to breaking.
Destroying items to release anything inside.
A unique mod feature that allows you to obtain items from others and break them, releasing anything inside.

In the server config, you can configure the stealing duration and who can be stolen from.
- "stealing_duration" is the duration in seconds that the key must be held to steal an item.
- "enable_universal_stealing" enables item stealing from all players all the time.
- "enable_sleep_stealing" enables item stealing from players while they sleep.
- "wake_up_chance" is the percent chance that a player will wake up while being stolen from.

You can steal items from other players by looking directly at them within range and holding down the Take/Break Item key.
As you hold the key, your theft progresses. Once complete, an item from their inventory is transferred directly into your hand!
This is especially helpful for retrieving stolen Miraculouses or capturing kamikotized objects.
book.mineraculous.wiki.items.cataclysm_d:
Blocks destroyed by Cataclysm are gradually converted into Cataclysm Blocks (a crumbly, sand-like substitute).
Mining a Cataclysm Block with a tool enchanted with Silk Touch will drop Cataclysm Dust instead of the block itself.
Without Silk Touch, the block simply breaks without dropping anything.
A rare remnant left behind by the destructive Cataclysm ability.

Cataclysm Dust is a rare byproduct of the Cataclysm ability used by the [Cat Miraculous](entry://miraculouses/cat).
Items struck by Cataclysm are converted directly into Cataclysm Dust, replacing them in the inventory.
Entities killed while under the Cataclysmed effect also drop Cataclysm Dust instead of their normal loot.
book.mineraculous.wiki.items.costumes.de: Cosmetic replica jewelry and suit armor found in ocean ruins.
book.mineraculous.wiki.items.costumes.ob:
While true magical Miraculouses are guarded far away in End Cities, replica jewelry and costume suit armor can be discovered right in the Overworld!
Intrepid divers and archaeologists can uncover them in ocean depths:
- **Underwater Ruin Chests** (Big and Small ruins)
- **Ocean Ruin Archaeology** (brushing suspicious sand and gravel in Cold and Warm ocean ruins)
book.mineraculous.wiki.items.costumes.ov:
Fake Miraculouses and Miraculous Costume Suits are cosmetic replicas of legendary armor and jewelry.
While they do not summon kwamis, grant attribute buffs, or unleash special abilities, they allow any player to dress up in iconic costume suits!
book.mineraculous.wiki.items.costumes.we:
To wear a Fake Miraculous, place it into your Head Curios slot just like normal jewelry.
Costume Armor pieces (mask, chestplate, leggings, and boots) can be equipped directly in your standard armor slots or in cosmetic override slots if a cosmetic mod is installed!
book.mineraculous.wiki.items.description: Unique items added by the mod, from combat tools to rare materials.
book.mineraculous.wiki.items.great_sword: A legendary blade summoned as a Lucky Charm against Ladybug Miraculous holders.

The Great Sword is a massive, epicly-tiered blade forged from miraculous-grade materials.
It deals exceptional damage (6 more than a base sword) but swings slower than standard swords.
It cannot be crafted or found in loot; it can only be obtained as a [Lucky Charm](entry://miraculouses/ladybug) by the Ladybug Miraculous holder when targeting another Ladybug Miraculous holder.
book.mineraculous.wiki.items.name: Items
book.mineraculous.wiki.items.rope.descri: A stronger, player-compatible replacement for the standard lead.
book.mineraculous.wiki.items.rope.name: Rope
book.mineraculous.wiki.items.rope.overvi:
Rope is an upgraded alternative to the standard lead.
Unlike a lead, Rope maintains a much tighter leash distance and pulls tied entities toward you with significantly more force.
It can also bind multiple entities together in a chain, and uniquely, it can even be used on other players.
book.mineraculous.wiki.items.rope.uses.t:
Rope has a variety of practical uses:
- Restraining mobs to prevent them from wandering
- Tying players together in cooperative or competitive scenarios
- Serving as a Lucky Charm for the [Ladybug Miraculous](entry://miraculouses/ladybug) when no specific target is present
book.mineraculous.wiki.kamikotizations.d: Special powers and costumes granted to players and mobs by the Butterfly Miraculous holder using Kamikos. Addons can also inject custom kamikotization entries into this category!
book.mineraculous.wiki.kamikotizations.k: General rules, mechanics, and features of all kamikotizations.

The only way to receive a kamikotization is from a [Kamiko](entry://getting_started/kamikos) sent by a [Butterfly Miraculous](entry://miraculouses/butterfly) holder.
The owner of the kamiko sends it to a target to access the contents of the target's inventory.
The owner will then choose a kamikotization to apply based on the items in the target's inventory,
disallowing that item to be moved while selection and communication occur.
The target has the option to accept the kamikotizations via a button or can reject the kamikotization with the Escape key if the server allows it.
If the owner selects themself, the Kamiko will wait for detransformation to begin the process.

Kamikotized entities cannot destroy their kamikotized item.
Instead, Kamikotizations can be rejected by the entity if the server allows it by pressing the Reject Kamikotization key.
This will release the kamiko from the item and remove the kamikotization and powers.

When a kamikotized entity is detransformed, the Kamiko will find a resting point and replicate.
The number of replicas depends on the server config.
Replicas will force minionize nearby players and despawn when all possible players are minionized.
This will also apply to Creative Mode players if enabled by the server config.
Minions will remain frozen until the original entity is re-kamikotized,
then act as tamed to the original.

Kamikotizations are revoked when the kamikotized item is destroyed or when the [Butterfly Miraculous](entry://miraculouses/butterfly) holder chooses to revoke the kamikotization.
This will release the kamiko from the damaged item and remove the kamikotization and powers.

The Toggle Buffs key can be pressed to toggle Kamikotization effect and attribute levels.
If enabled, effect levels scale with the Power Level of the Butterfly Miraculous holder who kamikotized you.
If disabled, effect levels will be capped to their starting baseline.
Whether these buffs are active immediately upon kamikotizing depends on the server config.

When kamikotized, the kamikotized item will either be transformed into a special tool or the target will be given an active ability to use.
These tools and abilities vary depending on the specific kamikotization applied.
Note: By default, the base Mineraculous mod comes with *no* built-in kamikotizations. Canonical kamikotizations from the show are included in the official **Kamikotization Expansion** addon, or can be added via custom datapacks and addons!
book.mineraculous.wiki.miraculouses.butt:
The Butterfly Miraculous grants three active abilities:
- [Kamikotization](entry://getting_started/kamikos)
- Kamiko Control
- Kamikotized Communication

Blade mode unsheathes a rapier-like blade that can be used to attack entities.
It can also be thrown, damaging any entity or item hit, and releasing a Kamiko if inside.

Block mode allows holding right click to spin the butterfly cane into a shield that will block most attacks and projectiles.
Holding the shield overhead allows slow fall.

The Butterfly Cane has five tool modes:
- Blade
- Block
- Kamiko Store
- Spyglass
- Throw

The Butterfly Miraculous is a brooch that, whenever Nooroo is inside, grants the wearer the power of Transmission.
Its tool is a cane and its signature ability is Kamikotization.
By default, the preferred food is small flowers and the treat is [hibiscus](entry://flora_and_fauna/flowers).

The Kamiko Control ability can be activated by pressing the Activate Power button with a [Kamiko](entry://getting_started/kamikos) nearby.
It will cause a mask to appear on the performer's face and will allow seeing through the eyes of the Kamiko.
The number keys then allow selecting a target that the Kamiko will fly to.
Once it reaches the target, it will open the Kamikotization Selection Screen and allow kamikotizing the target.
Kamiko Store mode allows right clicking to store or release a single Kamiko.

The [Kamikotization](entry://getting_started/kamikos) ability can be activated by pressing the Activate Power button with no [Kamikos](entry://getting_started/kamikos) nearby or stored.
It will cause particles to come from the performer's hand.
It will then convert any non-player entity into a Kamiko on interaction.

The Kamikotized Communication ability can be activated by pressing the Activate Power button with a kamikotized entity nearby.
It will cause a mask to appear on the performer's and target's face and will allow spectation, private chat, remote damage, and kamikotization revocation.

Spyglass mode allows using the cane as a spyglass to get a closer look at things.
It is activated by holding right click.

Throw mode allows right clicking to throw the cane.
This will damage any entity or item hit, releasing a Kamiko if inside.
The Cat Miraculous has three abilities:
- Cataclysm
- Cat Vision
- Passive Unluck

Block mode allows blocking incoming attacks by spinning the staff.
It will block incoming projectiles and melee attacks.

The Cat Vision ability is toggleable with the Toggle Special Effects key.
It will cause vision to become bright and green.

The Cataclysm ability can be activated by pressing the Activate Power button.
It will cause particles to come from the performer's hand and can be dragged for 1 second after use begins.
It can be used on items, blocks, and entities.
Items will be converted to [Cataclysm Dust](entry://items/cataclysm_dust).
Blocks will slowly convert themselves and adjacent blocks to Cataclysm Blocks,
which drop [Cataclysm Dust](entry://items/cataclysm_dust) when broken with Silk Touch.
Entities will be given the Cataclysmed effect that will slowly kill them,
converting drops to [Cataclysm Dust](entry://items/cataclysm_dust).
This is additive, so interacting with an entity multiple times will increase the Cataclysmed effect level.
It is possible to block Cataclysm with a shield,
which will apply it to the shield item instead of the target.

The Cat Miraculous is a ring that, whenever Plagg is inside, grants the wearer the power of Destruction.
Its tool is a staff and its signature ability is Cataclysm.
Charging the cat kwami requires [cheese](entry://food/cheese). By default, the normal food is any kind of cheese and the treat is camembert.

When targeting a Cat Miraculous holder,
the lucky charms can be one of the following:
- Any Shield
The Passive Unluck ability is a passive ability that reduces the luck attribute of all entities within a 10 block radius by 1.

Perch mode allows launching upwards by right clicking.
Right click can be pressed again to go back down,
or left click will make the staff fall in the facing direction,
or use the Ascend Tool and Descend Tool keys to change staff height.

Spyglass mode allows using the staff as a spyglass to get a closer look at things.
It is activated by holding right click.

The Cat Staff has five tool modes:
- Block
- Perch
- Spyglass
- Throw
- Travel

Throw mode allows right clicking to throw the staff.
This will damage any entity or item you hit, releasing a Kamiko if inside.

Travel mode allows launching forwards by right clicking.
Holding right click will launch further and further, up to a limit.
While in the air, right click can be held while looking down to slow fall or looking up to launch again. The user will be immune to fall damage while holding the staff.
book.mineraculous.wiki.miraculouses.comm:
The "charged" subcommand queries or sets the charged state of a kwami.
It requires the commands enabled permission level.
The kwami must be present to change its charged state.
The /miraculous command can be executed to configure per-player Miraculous data.

The "give" subcommand gives an item with the miraculous applied as the "mineraculous:miraculous" data component.
This is useful for cases like items, armor, tools, etc.
It functions similar to the normal "/give" command.
It requires the commands enabled permission level.

The "power_level" subcommand queries or sets the power level of a miraculous.
It requires the commands enabled permission level.
It has a maximum value of 100.
book.mineraculous.wiki.miraculouses.desc: Ancient magical jewelry inhabited by Kwamis that grant supernatural powers and unique tools.
book.mineraculous.wiki.miraculouses.gene:
To activate a Miraculous, equip it into its designated Curios slot by right-clicking it in your hand or shift-clicking it in your Curios menu.
Once equipped, its companion [Kwami](entry://miraculouses/kwamis) will spawn nearby to guide and assist you!

In survival mode, true magical Miraculouses can be discovered as rare endgame treasure within **End City Treasure Chests**!
Each Miraculous is a unique piece of jewelry tied to a specific concept and Kwami.
They can also be obtained via the Creative Menu under the Miraculouses tab, or through custom loot tables and recipes defined by modpacks.

After performing your main ability (if your Power Level is below 100), your Miraculous will begin flashing and beeping as a detransformation timer counts down.
When the timer expires, you will automatically detransform, and your exhausted Kwami will need to eat food before you can transform again.
As your Power Level increases through repeated use and progression, this timer will lengthen, and once you reach Power Level 100, the timer disappears entirely!

Every Miraculous tracks your Power Level (from 1 to 100), which increases as you perform abilities and gain experience.
Higher Power Levels grant stronger passive buffs and attribute bonuses.
Press the Toggle Buffs key to switch between your current scaled Power Level buffs and the baseline starting buffs.

With your Miraculous equipped and its Kwami charged, press the Transform button.
Your Kwami will merge into the jewelry, cloaking you in a magical suit of armor, granting superhuman attribute buffs (like speed, jump boost, and strength), and providing your signature tool.

While transformed, you can unleash your Miraculous's signature special ability by pressing the Activate Power button.
Be careful: if your Power Level is below 100, using your special ability consumes massive energy and will start your detransformation countdown timer!

Upon transforming, your signature tool (such as a Yoyo, Staff, or Cane) is placed in your inventory or Curios slot.
- Press the Open Item Menu button to quickly take or store your tool (if supported by that tool).
- Press the De/Activate Item button to toggle tool activation in hand (if supported by that tool).
- Hold the Open Item Menu button while holding the tool to open a radial menu and select different tool modes (such as Travel, Spyglass, or Block).
book.mineraculous.wiki.miraculouses.kwam:
After detransforming,
the kwami must be fed to recharge before it can be used to transform again.
To charge a kwami,
feed it with any food item,
or the kwami's preferred foods or treats.
Treats will charge the kwami immediately,
preferred foods have a 1 in 3 chance,
and other foods have a 1 in 10 chance.
Magic creatures summoned by the Miraculouses.

For better hiding, storage, and information,
the kwami can be right clicked with an empty hand to enter the inventory in item form.
While in the inventory, it can be hovered over to display information about it,
such as its charged state and food items.
It will eat preferred foods or treats in the inventory to recharge if enabled by the server config.
When transforming with its miraculous, the kwami will exit the inventory to assist.

Kwamis are summoned by their [Miraculous](category://miraculouses).
When summoned,
they will spin around in a ball of light.
While they are in this form,
they cannot be used to transform.

To renounce a kwami and keep the miraculous, press the Renounce button while holding the Miraculous in your hand.
To transfer or renounce to someone else, right click the kwami with the Miraculous in hand.

In order to transform,
the Kwami must be present and charged.
When transforming, the kwami will speed towards the miraculous in a ball of light and disappear.
book.mineraculous.wiki.miraculouses.lady:
The Ladybug Miraculous has three abilities:
- Lucky Charm
- Miraculous Ladybug
- Passive Luck

Because the tool is a Yoyo, it can't melee attack.
Instead, left clicking will launch a damaging projectile in the direction the user is facing.
This will damage any entity or item hit, releasing a Kamiko if inside.

Block mode allows holding right click to spin the yoyo in a shield that will block most attacks and projectiles.
Holding the shield upwards allows slow fall.

The Ladybug Miraculous are earrings that, whenever Tikki is inside, grant the wearer the power of Creation.
Its tool is a yoyo and its signature ability is Lucky Charm.
By default, the preferred food is bread and the treats are macarons, cookies, and cakes.

Lasso mode allows right clicking to throw the yoyo at any entity to keep it nearby,
even normally unleashable ones.
The yoyo can also be left clicked once an entity is attached to pull it towards the holder.
The length of the string can be controlled using the Ascend Tool and Descend Tool keys.

The Lucky Charm ability can be activated by pressing the Activate Power button with the active yoyo in hand.
This will summon a lucky charm item at the performer position.
If the tool is a special lucky charm summoning item, it can override the summon position.
The possible lucky charms are chosen from the loot table or list provided in the lucky charm [data maps](entry://apis/data_maps).
It will be tied to the relevant target used to determine the item.

Depending on the target or situation, different Lucky Charms can be summoned:
- Ladybug Miraculous Holder: [Great Sword](entry://items/great_sword)
- Warden: Distractor items (eggs, snowballs), Spectral Arrows (if holding a bow or crossbow), or a Milk Bucket (if suffering from Darkness)
- Elder Guardian: Sponge, Conduit, Prismarine, or a Milk Bucket (if suffering from Mining Fatigue)
- Generic (no specific target): [Rope](entry://items/rope), Apple, random tools, Rabbit's Foot, or golden foods

The Miraculous Ladybug ability can be activated by pressing the Activate Power button with the lucky charm in hand.
It will send the lucky charm into the air and summon magic ladybugs that fly around and heal all damage caused by miraculous or kamikotization abilities related to the target,
or the summoner of the Miraculous Ladybug if no target is specified.
There are many server and client config options for customization of this ability,
including one for altering the performance impact.

The Passive Luck ability needs no activation.
The Ladybug Miraculous holder automatically has the Luck effect applied while transformed.
This effect produces better loot when opening containers or fishing.
The effect level scales as Power Level increases.

Purify mode allows capturing and purifying Kamikos with left click.
There is no limit to how many Kamikos can be captured,
and right clicking will release all stored Kamikos at once,
launching them upwards.
When no Kamikos are stored, right clicking causes the yoyo to spin as a shield.

Spyglass mode allows using the yoyo as a spyglass to get a closer look at things.
It is activated by holding right click.

Travel mode allows using the yoyo to swing on blocks and launch into the air.
Right click throws and recalls the yoyo and left click launches the user.

The Ladybug Yoyo has a unique attack functionality and five tool modes:
- Block
- Lasso
- Purify
- Spyglass
- Travel
book.mineraculous.wiki.miraculouses.name: Miraculouses
book.mineraculous.wiki.name: Mineraculous Wiki
book.mineraculous.wiki.support.contact.d: Locations to contact the mod and its developers.

For questions, discussions, and feature requests,
come chat with us on [Discord](https://discord.gg/Vd6yX2ngWX).
book.mineraculous.wiki.support.contact.e:
For business inquiries and other matters,
you can reach out to [mineraculous@thomasglasser.dev](mailto:mineraculous@thomasglasser.dev).
book.mineraculous.wiki.support.contact.g:
For issues and bugs,
make an issue on [GitHub](https://github.com/thomasglasser/Mineraculous/issues).
book.mineraculous.wiki.support.descripti: Finding, supporting, and getting help with Mineraculous.
book.mineraculous.wiki.support.mod_pages: The mod can also be found on [CurseForge](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/mineraculous).
The locations the mod can be found and downloaded.

The source code for the mod can be found on [GitHub](https://github.com/thomasglasser/Mineraculous).
This is also the place to report issues and bugs and find base models for addons.
The main place to find and download the mod is on [Modrinth](https://modrinth.com/mod/mineraculous).

This wiki contains information about the mod and its features.
